writeup: Friday, June 12 marked a tidy administrative milestone for Buffalo as the Bills officially signed fourth-round offensive tackle Jude Bowry to a four-year rookie deal, completing the signing of all 10 members of their 2026 draft class. The move came on the heels of the team wrapping up mandatory minicamp, where new head coach Joe Brady's revamped staff — featuring a new offensive coordinator, defensive coordinator, and special teams coach — got valuable reps in a contact-free but critically important setting. The Bills also made five roster moves as minicamp closed, including placing RB Desmond Reid on injured reserve, waiving WR Gabriel Benyard, and reverting DB Dorian Strong to the reserve/non-football injury list. Against that backdrop, national media weighed in on Buffalo's prospects under Brady, with ClutchPoints noting that 'a coaching change, a revamped roster, and heightened expectations have created an entirely new landscape in Western New York,' while SI ranked the Bills as the top Super Bowl contender among franchises still chasing their first Lombardi Trophy.
